message:
perf(storage): update_issue is a no-op when no field actually changes

Before this change, calling update_issue with an IssueUpdate whose
fields all matched the current row still:

- rewrote updated_at on the row (always, unconditionally),
- marked the issue dirty in the export-hash bookkeeping, and
- for the priority branch specifically: recorded a PriorityChanged
  history event with old == new even when the new value was
  identical.

That turned intended-as-idempotent "re-apply the same set" calls
(agents reasserting a state, UI flows re-saving unchanged form
fields, import paths replaying JSONL) into real writes that
dirtied pages and polluted history. Paired with the round-3
byte-identical auto-flush and no-op export-hash fast paths this
was the last layer still forcing a rewrite on semantically-identical
input.

Two surgical fixes in the update_issue transaction:

1. Priority branch: wrap the `issue.priority = priority`,
   add_update("priority", …), and record_field_change(
   PriorityChanged, …) calls in the pre-existing
   `if priority.0 != old_priority { … }` check. The condition
   existed for the history-event record; now it also gates the
   SET clause so an unchanged priority stops producing a dirty
   row. Matches the shape other fields (status / assignee /
   description) already use.

2. At the end of the field-assembly block, add an early return
   when set_clauses is empty. All no-op branches (including the
   newly-gated priority branch) fall through without touching
   updated_at, skipping the UPDATE against the issues table, and
   skipping the dirty-marker append. Before, the "Always update
   updated_at" comment was literal — now the comment reads
   "Update updated_at only when a stored field needs rewriting."

New unit test test_update_issue_same_priority_is_noop:
- creates an issue at priority HIGH,
- clears all dirty markers,
- calls update_issue with IssueUpdate { priority: Some(HIGH), .. },
- asserts the returned row is unchanged (same updated_at, same
  priority), and
- asserts clear_all_dirty_issues is still empty afterwards — the
  write path produced zero dirty markers.

No external API change: the IssueUpdate surface is unchanged and
callers that legitimately want to bump updated_at still use
touch_issue / the explicit "updated_at = Utc::now()" helpers.

Co-Authored-By: Claude <noreply@anthropic.com>
